subject: Civics
chapter: Parliament and the Making of Laws

activities_projects.title: Detailed Law-Making Process Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand and analyse the stages involved in the law-making process
steps: Read the section explaining how laws are made in Parliament
Write the steps involved from introducing a bill to passing the law
Explain why discussion and voting are important in this process
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyses the stages of the law-making process
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and depth of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Case Study: Public Issue to Law
activities_projects.objective: Understand how public issues influence the creation of laws
steps: Choose a public issue such as pollution, safety or education
Explain how people can raise the issue
Describe how Parliament might make a law to address it
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies knowledge of parliamentary processes to real-life situations

activities_projects.title: Debate on the Role of Parliament
activities_projects.objective: Develop critical thinking about the role of Parliament in democracy
steps: Organise a class debate on the topic 'Parliament represents the voice of the people'
Prepare arguments supporting or opposing the statement
Write a summary of the main points discussed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the importance of parliamentary representation
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of arguments and summary (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparing Parliament with Local Government
steps: List the functions of Parliament
List the functions of local government bodies such as municipalities or panchayats
Compare how their roles are similar and different
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the structure of governance in India

activities_projects.title: Critical Thinking: Why Laws Change
activities_projects.objective: Understand that laws change according to society's needs
steps: Write two examples where a law may need to change over time
Explain why the change is necessary
Describe the role of Parliament in making such changes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognises the evolving nature of laws

activities_projects.title: Question Hour Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the role of Question Hour in ensuring government accountability
steps: Read about Question Hour in Parliament
Explain how MPs use Question Hour to question the government
Write why this process is important in democracy
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the accountability mechanisms in Parliament

activities_projects.title: Mock Parliamentary Session
activities_projects.objective: Experience the process of parliamentary decision-making
steps: Assign roles such as Speaker, MPs, and ministers
Introduce a topic such as environmental protection
Conduct a discussion and vote to pass or reject the proposed rule
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands parliamentary debate and voting procedures
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and understanding of roles (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Research on an Important Law
activities_projects.objective: Understand the impact of laws passed by Parliament
steps: Choose a law passed by Parliament such as Right to Education
Write what the law states
Explain how the law benefits people
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the social impact of legislation

activities_projects.title: Analysis of Parliamentary Representation
activities_projects.objective: Understand how Parliament represents diverse groups
steps: Write about how MPs represent different regions of India
Explain why representation is important in democracy
Give examples of issues MPs may raise for their constituencies
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and depth of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Essay on the Role of Parliament in Democracy
activities_projects.objective: Summarise the importance of Parliament in governance
steps: Write an essay explaining the role of Parliament in a democratic country
Include points about representation, law-making and accountability
Conclude with why Parliament is essential for democracy
